title: What are Functions?
description: Stop repeating yourself! Wrap code in reusable blocks.

slides.title: Don't Repeat Yourself (DRY) 🏜️
slides.content:
## Reusable Machines

If you find yourself writing the exact same 10 lines of code in 5 different places... you are doing it wrong!

Instead, you should wrap those lines of code up into a single named block called a **Function**.
If variables are boxes that hold *data*, functions are boxes that hold *actions*.

quizData.question: What is the primary real-world benefit of using functions?

options.text: They make the program run faster
They allow you to reuse code without typing it over and over

quizData.explanation: Functions embody the DRY principle (Don't Repeat Yourself). Write it once, use it universally.

slides.title: The 'func' Keyword ⚙️
slides.content:
In Go, we define a function using the keyword **`func`**.

You already know one function perfectly: `func main()`! Let's make our own custom one.

quizData.question: To create a function in Go, what keyword do you use? _____
quizData.explanation: Go uses the shortened `func` keyword to declare functions.
